title: Malty Creamy Russian Fish Pilaf
description: A creamy, malty, and indulgent pilaf from Russia, perfect for a weekend brunch. With the added umami from nam pla, this dish is sure to satisfy.
instructions: Heat the ghee or oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and sauté until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the caraway seeds and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the cod fillets and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until lightly browned.
Add the fish sauce, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the uncooked rice and stir to coat with the fish sauce mixture.
Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the rice is cooked and the liquid has been absorbed.
In a separate saucepan, combine the malt vinegar and heavy cream. Bring to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and let simmer for 5 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Stir in the chopped parsley and dill.
To serve, place a portion of the pilaf on a plate, then spoon the creamy sauce over the top.
